title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · Result-Grain Case: Relation Domain
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql relation domain increment in a key-and-null-aware relational model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.
Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 6 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships. A defensible solution applies Relation Domain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a key-and-null-aware relational model.

content.code:
SELECT t.ticket_id, t.owner_id, m.name AS owner_name
FROM tickets AS t
LEFT JOIN members AS m ON m.member_id = t.owner_id
WHERE t.owner_id IS NULL OR m.active = 1
ORDER BY t.ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
